bullets.text: Operating margin fell 5.7 points year-over-year; Q2 personal auto combined ratio of 92.5, slightly below Q1's 92.9; net income declined 29% versus Q2 2006, reflecting planned rate reductions and adverse reserve adjustments.

Written and earned premium fell year-over-year; average earned premium per car year down about 4% for both Agency and Direct, and 5% for Commercial Auto; written premium per unit declining 1–2 percentage points faster than earned.

Surpassed 10 million Personal Lines customers during Q2; Direct auto PIF up 5% year-over-year; Agency auto PIF running slightly below last year; new business applications in Personal Lines increased 3% for Q2 and 2% year-to-date.

Unexpected uninsured motorist reserve adjustment in Special Lines and Commercial Auto added to Q2 results; some additional unfavorable prior-year loss development on larger personal and commercial auto losses also recorded.

Policy life expectancy continued to improve in most businesses and tiers through Q2; Net Promoter Score showed steady gains each of the last six months, attributed to rate actions and improved claims and servicing experiences.

Advertising spend increased year-over-year with a shift toward Internet and television and away from direct mail; consumer research showed improving awareness of Progressive's service advantages and competitive prices.
Internet sales represented 60–65% of Direct new business; new business growth returned in 32 states for Agency auto and continues to grow faster than any other segment in Direct via Internet.
